fertilise.notes: Citrus-specific fertiliser (e.g. Yates Citrus Food). Spring and summer are most important.

prune.notes: Light shaping after main fruiting season. Remove dead wood and crossing branches.

check_pests.notes: Watch for: scale insects (brown bumps), leafminer (squiggly trails on leaves), aphids.

deep_water.notes: Deep soak to encourage deep root growth. Reduce in winter. Ensure good drainage.

mulch.notes: Apply 5–10cm of organic mulch, keeping clear of trunk.

fertilise.notes: Citrus fertiliser, spring through autumn. Avoid high nitrogen in winter.

prune.notes: Prune lightly after harvest. Remove suckers from below graft union.

check_pests.notes: Scale, leafminer, fruit fly in late summer.

deep_water.notes: Regular deep watering, especially during fruiting.

fertilise.notes: Rose-specific fertiliser from spring to autumn. Stop 6 weeks before first frost.

prune.notes: Hard prune in winter (July in Australia). Deadhead spent flowers throughout flowering season.

spray_fungicide.notes: Preventative spray for black spot and powdery mildew. Use copper-based fungicide.

check_pests.notes: Aphids, thrips, two-spotted mite. Check undersides of leaves.

arborist_check.notes: Have a qualified arborist inspect for structural integrity, especially near structures or fences.

prune.notes: Minimal pruning — remove dead or hazardous limbs only. Avoid pruning in late summer (attract borers).

check_pests.notes: Psyllids, longicorn borers, lerps. Look for yellowing or dying branches.

prune.notes: Remove spent flower stalks. Divide clumps every 3–5 years when flowering declines.

water.notes: Drought tolerant once established but flowers better with occasional summer water.

prune.notes: Trim by one-third after flowering to maintain shape and prevent woody base.

fertilise.notes: Low fertiliser needs — light organic feed in spring only.

check_drainage.notes: Lavender hates wet roots. Ensure excellent drainage.

fertilise.notes: Slow-release fertiliser in spring and summer.

prune.notes: Trim for shape after flowering. Tolerates hard pruning.

water.notes: Regular watering especially in heat. Mulch to retain moisture.

fertilise.notes: High-phosphorus fertiliser (e.g. Osmocote Plus) in spring and summer to encourage flowers.

prune.notes: Prune in late winter before new growth. Milky sap — wear gloves.

check_rust.notes: Frangipani rust (orange spots on leaves) is common. Spray with fungicide if severe.

water.notes: Drought tolerant. Reduce watering in winter — risk of root rot.

fertilise.notes: Camellia/azalea fertiliser (acid-loving plants). Spring after flowering finishes.

prune.notes: Immediately after flowering. Remove spent blooms to prevent petal blight spread.

mulch.notes: Acidic mulch (pine bark) to maintain soil pH around 5.5–6.5.

water.notes: Regular water especially during bud formation (autumn). Never let roots dry out.

prune.notes: Prune twice: summer (trim new shoots to 5 leaves) and winter (cut back hard to 2–3 buds).

fertilise.notes: Low nitrogen — promotes foliage over flowers. Use a tomato fertiliser.

check_structure.notes: Check attachment points — wisteria is extremely heavy when mature and can damage structures.

water.notes: Frequent watering especially in pots or during establishment.

fertilise.notes: High nitrogen fertiliser spring through summer.

contain.notes: Running bamboo — check for rhizome escape. Install root barrier if not already present.

mow.notes: During growing season. Raise mowing height in summer to reduce water stress.

fertilise.notes: Lawn fertiliser spring through summer. Don't fertilise in drought.

aerate.notes: Aerate compacted areas each spring. Helps water penetration.

weed.notes: Spot-treat bindii and broadleaf weeds before seeding.

water.notes: Deep, infrequent watering encourages deep roots. Check local water restrictions.

check_health.notes: General visual inspection: yellowing, wilting, pests, unusual growth.

prune.notes: Annual light prune — remove dead or damaged growth.

water.notes: Regular watering during establishment (first year). Reduce once established.

mulch.notes: Annual top-up of organic mulch to retain moisture and suppress weeds.
